title: Exchanges vs OTC: Options are typically traded in over-the-counter markets, which do not...
summary: Compare exchange-traded and OTC-traded derivatives regarding regulation and risk.
prompt: Options are typically traded in over-the-counter markets, which do not have the same regulatory structure as exchanges.
solution: True; the OTC market operates with fewer regulations compared to organized exchanges, making it crucial for participants to understand the credit risk associated with counterparties.
